statements.rationale: Session configuration statement that suppresses row-count messages; dbt does not require this and it carries no transformation logic.

statements.sql: DECLARE @Today AS DATE = CAST(GETDATE() AS DATE)
statements.rationale: Variable declaration used only to parameterise the SCD date values; no data transformation — dbt expressions replace this inline.
Operational row-count accumulators used solely for the PRINT logging statement; carry no transformation logic.

statements.sql: UPDATE dw SET dw.EffectiveEnd = DATEADD(DAY, -1, @Today), dw.IsCurrent = 0 FROM dbo.Dim_Employee AS dw INNER JOIN PTP_System.Master.Employees AS src ON dw.Employee_BK = src.EmployeeCode AND dw.IsCurrent = 1 WHERE dw.SourceRowHash <> HASHBYTES('SHA2_256', CONCAT_WS('|', src.FullName, src.Email, src.Department, src.JobTitle, CAST(src.IsActive AS CHAR(1))))
statements.rationale: SCD Type 2 expiry phase — core transformation logic that closes changed employee rows by setting EffectiveEnd and IsCurrent=0; must be represented in the dbt model.
Captures the UPDATE row count into a local variable used only for PRINT logging; no transformation logic.

statements.sql: INSERT INTO dbo.Dim_Employee (Employee_BK, FullName, Email, Department, JobTitle, IsActive, EffectiveStart, EffectiveEnd, IsCurrent, DW_InsertDate, SourceRowHash) SELECT src.EmployeeCode, src.FullName, src.Email, src.Department, src.JobTitle, src.IsActive, @Today, '9999-12-31', 1, SYSUTCDATETIME(), HASHBYTES('SHA2_256', CONCAT_WS('|', src.FullName, src.Email, src.Department, src.JobTitle, CAST(src.IsActive AS CHAR(1)))) FROM PTP_System.Master.Employees src WHERE NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM dbo.Dim_Employee dw WHERE dw.Employee_BK = src.EmployeeCode AND dw.IsCurrent = 1)
statements.rationale: SCD Type 2 insert phase — core transformation logic that inserts new and replacement employee rows from the source system; must be represented in the dbt model.
Captures the INSERT row count into a local variable used only for PRINT logging; no transformation logic.
Logging-only PRINT statement that reports operational row counts; carries no transformation logic and is irrelevant in dbt.
